elements.title: Madalitso Baloyi
elements.contents: ... that Madalitso Baloyi (pictured), Malawi's minister of health, pretended to be a patient at a hospital in Lilongwe?

elements.title: a 1975 Turkish short story
elements.contents: ... that a critic compared the way in which the protagonist of a 1975 Turkish short story isolated himself out of fear to the COVID-19 lockdowns?

elements.title: Madeline Woo
elements.contents: ... that Madeline Woo, a San Francisco Ballet principal dancer, has tattoos but covers them for performances?

elements.title: a Hawaii TV station
elements.contents: ... that a Hawaii TV station telecast a football game as a favor to the state's dying former governor?

elements.title: Aria Bolkus
elements.contents: ... that Aria Bolkus, the daughter of a former government minister, was elected to the South Australian House of Assembly three months after her father's death?

elements.title: The Executioner and Her Way of Life
elements.contents: ... that a character's outfit from the anime The Executioner and Her Way of Life was inspired by Clint Eastwood's spaghetti Western roles?

elements.title: Fedor Karzhavin
elements.contents: ... that Fedor Karzhavin may have stolen the charter of the College of William & Mary?

elements.title: Forest Service Road System
elements.contents: ... that the Forest Service Road System is eight times the length of the Interstate Highway System?

elements.title: Henry I of Castile
elements.contents: ... that, after 13-year-old King Henry I of Castile was fatally struck by a tile dislodged by a playmate, his guardian tried to hide his death?
